rawText:
Live sketch comedy has made an unexpected return to British television with the launch of a new weekly variety show, the first of its kind in over a decade. The inaugural episode, hosted by a veteran American comedy writer and performer, drew mixed reviews but generated significant attention on social media, where clips quickly accumulated hundreds of thousands of views. Producers positioned the program as a necessary release valve during a period of geopolitical tension and political polarization. The format relies on the unpredictability of live broadcast, a rarity in an era when most comedy is pre-recorded, and its cast includes several writers and performers who first gained followings online. If the show can consistently convert viral moments into loyal viewership, it may help revive a genre that had all but disappeared from British screens.

The appetite for humor arrives at a moment when the English-speaking world appears to need it most. For the second consecutive year, no country from the Anglosphere ranked among the top ten in the annual global happiness survey, even as Finland claimed the top position for the ninth time in a row. The decline is sharpest among young people: in six major English-speaking nations, life satisfaction scores for those under twenty-five have fallen notably over the past decade. Social media is a frequently cited explanation, since the timing coincides with the spread of smartphones and algorithmic platforms. Yet the relationship is not straightforward, as countries in other regions with equally heavy social media usage report stable or improving wellbeing among the young.

A different kind of cultural shift is playing out in the economics of intimate services, a sector that generates an estimated one hundred billion dollars annually worldwide yet receives remarkably little attention from researchers. A single subscription platform hosting millions of adult content creators processes over seven billion dollars in annual spending, yet fewer than five percent of the academic literature on the broader industry over the past quarter century has adopted an economic lens. Digital platforms have begun to transform this market, lowering barriers to entry and making aspects of the trade more visible to study. Researchers have borrowed creative methods from ecology, including tagging and recapturing participants at known locations, to estimate population sizes in the absence of reliable official data. Natural experiments created by changing regulations have also yielded insights, with studies finding that decriminalization tends to reduce both violent crime and disease transmission.

These three developments reflect a broader pattern in which social norms, emotional wellbeing, and economic activity are evolving faster than the institutions designed to measure and govern them.

overview.title: Cultural Crosscurrents: Comedy, Happiness, and Hidden Economies

sentences.original: Live sketch comedy has made an unexpected return to British television with the launch of a new weekly variety show, the first of its kind in over a decade.
sentences.translation: 라이브 스케치 코미디가 새로운 주간 버라이어티 쇼의 출범과 함께 영국 텔레비전에 예기치 않은 복귀를 했는데, 이는 10년 넘게 그 종류로는 최초이다.

grammarElements.text: an unexpected return to British television
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: with the launch of a new weekly variety show
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 수식어

grammarElements.text: the first of its kind in over a decade
grammarElements.label: 동격 보충어

vocabulary.example: Saturday Night Live is America's most famous sketch comedy show.
The inaugural flight of the new airline was fully booked.

expressions.usage: 사라졌다가 다시 나타나는 것을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has made an unexpected return to [field/place]
patterns.explanation: 한동안 사라졌던 것이 예상치 못하게 다시 등장했음을 나타내는 패턴
examples: Vinyl records have made an unexpected return to mainstream music.
The classic board game has made an unexpected return to family entertainment.

drillQuestions.question: Live sketch comedy has made an unexpected ______ to British television.

sentences.notes: 현재완료 시제로 최근 변화를 보도하고, 동격 구문으로 그 중요성을 즉시 부연한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Live sketch comedy
has made an unexpected return
with the launch of a new weekly variety show
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 주간 버라이어티 쇼의 출범과 함께

koreanAlignment.en: the first of its kind in over a decade

sentences.original: The inaugural episode, hosted by a veteran American comedy writer and performer, drew mixed reviews but generated significant attention on social media, where clips quickly accumulated hundreds of thousands of views.
sentences.translation: 베테랑 미국 코미디 작가이자 연기자가 진행한 첫 번째 에피소드는 엇갈린 평가를 받았지만 소셜 미디어에서 상당한 관심을 불러일으켰고, 클립들은 빠르게 수십만 조회수를 기록했다.

grammarElements.text: hosted by a veteran American comedy writer and performer
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식구

grammarElements.text: where clips quickly accumulated hundreds of thousands of views
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절

vocabulary.example: The veteran journalist covered the story with remarkable depth.
The video accumulated over a million views within a week.

expressions.usage: 긍정과 부정 반응이 섞였을 때 사용하는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] drew mixed reviews but generated [positive outcome]
patterns.explanation: 비평적 평가와 실제 대중적 반응이 다를 때 대비시키는 패턴
examples: The film drew mixed reviews but generated record box-office sales.
The restaurant drew mixed reviews but generated a loyal local following.

drillQuestions.question: The inaugural episode drew ______ reviews but generated significant attention on social media.

sentences.notes: but으로 비평과 대중 반응의 대조를 만들고, where절로 소셜 미디어 파급력을 부연한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The inaugural episode
hosted by a veteran American comedy writer and performer
koreanAlignment.ko: 베테랑 미국 코미디 작가이자 연기자가 진행한

koreanAlignment.en: drew mixed reviews but generated significant attention
koreanAlignment.ko: 엇갈린 평가를 받았지만 상당한 관심을 불러일으켰다

sentences.original: Producers positioned the program as a necessary release valve during a period of geopolitical tension and political polarization.
sentences.translation: 제작진은 이 프로그램을 지정학적 긴장과 정치적 양극화 시기에 필요한 해소 수단으로 포지셔닝했다.

grammarElements.text: during a period of geopolitical tension and political polarization
grammarElements.label: 시간 전치사구

vocabulary.example: Sports serve as a release valve for the pressures of daily life.
Political polarization has made bipartisan legislation increasingly rare.

expressions.usage: 마케팅이나 전략적 맥락에서 특정 이미지를 부여할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 특정 상품이나 프로그램을 전략적으로 자리매김하는 패턴
examples: The company positioned the product as an affordable luxury during the economic downturn.
The government positioned the policy as a safety net during the transition period.

drillQuestions.question: Producers positioned the program as a necessary release ______ during a period of geopolitical tension.

sentences.notes: position A as B 구문은 마케팅·저널리즘에서 자주 쓰이는 핵심 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Producers positioned the program
a necessary release valve
geopolitical tension and political polarization

sentences.original: The format relies on the unpredictability of live broadcast, a rarity in an era when most comedy is pre-recorded, and its cast includes several writers and performers who first gained followings online.
sentences.translation: 이 포맷은 대부분의 코미디가 사전 녹화되는 시대에 희귀한 생방송의 예측불가능성에 의존하며, 출연진에는 처음 온라인에서 팔로워를 확보한 여러 작가와 연기자가 포함된다.

grammarElements.text: a rarity in an era when most comedy is pre-recorded
grammarElements.label: 동격 보충어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: and its cast includes several writers and performers who first gained followings online
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The unpredictability of the weather made outdoor planning difficult.
A truly bipartisan agreement is a rarity in today's political landscape.

expressions.usage: 소셜 미디어에서 지지자 기반을 구축했음을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] relies on [quality], a rarity in an era when [contrast]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 특성에 의존한다는 것을 현 시대와 대비하며 강조하는 패턴
examples: The restaurant relies on seasonal ingredients, a rarity in an era when most chains use frozen supplies.
The school relies on handwritten exams, a rarity in an era when most testing is digital.

drillQuestions.question: The format relies on the ______ of live broadcast, a rarity in an era when most comedy is pre-recorded.

sentences.notes: 동격 삽입구 'a rarity in an era when ...'으로 생방송의 희소성을 효과적으로 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The format relies on the unpredictability of live broadcast
a rarity in an era when most comedy is pre-recorded
koreanAlignment.ko: 대부분의 코미디가 사전 녹화되는 시대에 희귀한

koreanAlignment.en: writers and performers who first gained followings online
koreanAlignment.ko: 처음 온라인에서 팔로워를 확보한 작가와 연기자

sentences.original: If the show can consistently convert viral moments into loyal viewership, it may help revive a genre that had all but disappeared from British screens.
sentences.translation: 만약 이 쇼가 바이럴 순간들을 꾸준히 충성도 높은 시청자층으로 전환할 수 있다면, 영국 화면에서 거의 사라졌던 장르를 되살리는 데 도움이 될 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: If the show can consistently convert viral moments into loyal viewership
grammarElements.label: 조건절

grammarElements.text: a genre that had all but disappeared from British screens
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절 (과거완료)

vocabulary.example: The company struggled to convert free users into paying customers.
The new manager hopes to revive the struggling franchise.

expressions.usage: 완전히는 아니지만 거의 없어진 상태를 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: If [subject] can consistently convert [A] into [B], it may help [outcome]
patterns.explanation: 전환 가능성과 그 결과를 조건부로 제시하는 패턴
examples: If the platform can consistently convert free trials into subscriptions, it may help reach profitability.
If the university can consistently convert applicants into enrolled students, it may help solve the funding crisis.

drillQuestions.question: If the show can consistently ______ viral moments into loyal viewership, it may help revive the genre.
It may help revive a genre that had all ______ disappeared from British screens.

sentences.notes: 'all but disappeared'는 '거의 완전히 사라진'이라는 뜻으로, all but + 과거분사는 고급 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: consistently convert viral moments into loyal viewership
koreanAlignment.ko: 바이럴 순간들을 꾸준히 충성도 높은 시청자층으로 전환하다

koreanAlignment.en: revive a genre
had all but disappeared from British screens

sentences.original: The appetite for humor arrives at a moment when the English-speaking world appears to need it most.
sentences.translation: 유머에 대한 갈망은 영어권 세계가 가장 필요로 하는 것처럼 보이는 시점에 도래한다.

grammarElements.text: when the English-speaking world appears to need it most
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절

vocabulary.example: There is growing appetite among investors for sustainable energy stocks.

expressions.usage: 어떤 현상이 특정 시기와 맞물려 나타남을 강조

patterns.explanation: 특정 현상이 시의적절한 맥락에서 나타남을 강조하는 저널리즘 패턴
examples: The push for renewable energy arrives at a moment when fossil fuel prices are at record highs.
The privacy debate arrives at a moment when governments are expanding surveillance powers.

drillQuestions.question: The appetite for humor arrives at a ______ when the English-speaking world appears to need it most.

sentences.notes: appetite를 음식이 아닌 추상적 갈망으로 사용하는 비유적 용법에 주목하자.

koreanAlignment.en: The appetite for humor
arrives at a moment
the English-speaking world appears to need it most
koreanAlignment.ko: 영어권 세계가 가장 필요로 하는 것처럼 보이는

sentences.original: For the second consecutive year, no country from the Anglosphere ranked among the top ten in the annual global happiness survey, even as Finland claimed the top position for the ninth time in a row.
sentences.translation: 2년 연속으로 영어권 국가 중 어느 나라도 연간 세계 행복 설문조사에서 상위 10위 안에 들지 못했으며, 같은 기간 핀란드는 9년 연속 1위를 차지했다.

grammarElements.text: the top ten in the annual global happiness survey
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: even as Finland claimed the top position for the ninth time in a row
grammarElements.label: 양보절

vocabulary.example: The team won three consecutive championships.
The Anglosphere includes countries like the UK, the US, Canada, and Australia.

expressions.phrase: for the ninth time in a row
expressions.usage: 연속적 성과나 기록을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: For the [ordinal] consecutive [period], no [group] [verb], even as [contrast]
patterns.explanation: 부정적 추세를 연속 기간으로 강조하면서 대조적 사례를 병치하는 패턴
examples: For the third consecutive quarter, no major bank reported rising profits, even as the economy expanded.
For the fifth consecutive year, no domestic airline ranked among the safest, even as global accident rates declined.

drillQuestions.question: For the second ______ year, no country from the Anglosphere ranked among the top ten.

sentences.notes: even as 양보절로 핀란드의 안정적 1위와 영어권의 추락을 극적으로 대비한다.

koreanAlignment.en: For the second consecutive year
no country from the Anglosphere
ranked among the top ten
Finland claimed the top position for the ninth time in a row

sentences.original: The decline is sharpest among young people: in six major English-speaking nations, life satisfaction scores for those under twenty-five have fallen notably over the past decade.
sentences.translation: 하락세는 젊은이들 사이에서 가장 두드러진다: 6개 주요 영어권 국가에서 25세 미만의 삶의 만족도 점수가 지난 10년간 눈에 띄게 하락했다.

grammarElements.text: in six major English-speaking nations, life satisfaction scores for those under twenty-five have fallen notably over the past decade
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 부연절

vocabulary.example: Unemployment rose notably in the manufacturing sector.
Researchers measured life satisfaction across different age groups.

expressions.usage: 장기적 추세를 서술할 때 사용하는 시간 표현

patterns.explanation: 특정 집단에서 추세가 가장 극명하게 나타남을 콜론으로 구체화하는 패턴
examples: The decline is sharpest among rural communities: three out of five small towns have lost more than a quarter of their population.
The growth is sharpest among millennials: consumer spending by under-40s has tripled in five years.

drillQuestions.question: The decline is sharpest among young people: life satisfaction scores have fallen ______ over the past decade.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:)을 사용해 일반적 주장 뒤에 구체적 데이터를 제시하는 저널리즘의 전형적 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The decline is sharpest among young people
life satisfaction scores
have fallen notably over the past decade

sentences.original: Social media is a frequently cited explanation, since the timing coincides with the spread of smartphones and algorithmic platforms.
sentences.translation: 소셜 미디어가 자주 인용되는 설명인데, 시기적으로 스마트폰과 알고리즘 기반 플랫폼의 확산과 일치하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: since the timing coincides with the spread of smartphones and algorithmic platforms
grammarElements.label: 이유절

vocabulary.example: The release of the report coincided with the start of the election campaign.
Algorithmic trading now accounts for most stock market transactions.

expressions.usage: 연구나 논의에서 반복적으로 등장하는 논점을 소개할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Factor] is a frequently cited explanation, since [correlation]
patterns.explanation: 상관관계를 근거로 자주 거론되는 설명을 소개하는 패턴
examples: Climate change is a frequently cited explanation, since the warming trend coincides with rising CO2 levels.
Screen time is a frequently cited explanation, since the decline in reading coincides with the rise of streaming.

drillQuestions.question: Social media is a frequently cited explanation, since the timing ______ with the spread of smartphones.

sentences.notes: since절은 '~이기 때문에'라는 이유를 나타내며, '~이후로'라는 시간 의미와 구별해야 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Social media is a frequently cited explanation
the timing coincides with
the spread of smartphones and algorithmic platforms

sentences.original: Yet the relationship is not straightforward, as countries in other regions with equally heavy social media usage report stable or improving wellbeing among the young.
sentences.translation: 하지만 그 관계는 단순하지 않은데, 소셜 미디어 사용량이 동등하게 높은 다른 지역의 국가들은 젊은층의 안녕감이 안정적이거나 개선되고 있다고 보고하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: as countries in other regions with equally heavy social media usage report stable or improving wellbeing among the young
grammarElements.label: 이유절

vocabulary.example: The answer is not as straightforward as it might seem.
The government launched a new initiative to improve the wellbeing of elderly citizens.

expressions.usage: 비교 대상이 같은 수준임을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Yet the relationship is not straightforward, as [counter-evidence]
patterns.explanation: 앞서 제시된 상관관계에 반론을 제기하는 패턴
examples: Yet the relationship is not straightforward, as some high-income countries report lower happiness than their poorer neighbors.
Yet the relationship is not straightforward, as increased policing does not always correlate with reduced crime.

drillQuestions.question: Yet the relationship is not ______, as countries in other regions report stable wellbeing.

sentences.notes: Yet으로 시작해 앞 문장의 상관관계가 인과관계가 아님을 지적하는 비판적 사고의 전형이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet the relationship is not straightforward
countries in other regions with equally heavy social media usage
koreanAlignment.ko: 소셜 미디어 사용량이 동등하게 높은 다른 지역의 국가들

koreanAlignment.en: report stable or improving wellbeing among the young
koreanAlignment.ko: 젊은층의 안녕감이 안정적이거나 개선되고 있다고 보고하다

sentences.original: A different kind of cultural shift is playing out in the economics of intimate services, a sector that generates an estimated one hundred billion dollars annually worldwide yet receives remarkably little attention from researchers.
sentences.translation: 또 다른 종류의 문화적 변화가 친밀 서비스 경제학에서 전개되고 있는데, 이 분야는 전 세계적으로 연간 약 1,000억 달러를 창출하면서도 연구자들로부터 놀라울 정도로 적은 관심을 받고 있다.

grammarElements.text: A different kind of cultural shift
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: in the economics of intimate services
grammarElements.label: 전치사구

grammarElements.text: a sector that generates ... yet receives ...
grammarElements.label: 동격어 + 관계절 (역접)

vocabulary.example: The political drama is playing out on the national stage.
The economics of intimate services remain understudied in academia.

patterns.pattern: [Phenomenon] is playing out in [domain], a sector that [generates X] yet [receives Y]
patterns.explanation: 변화가 전개되는 분야를 소개하면서 규모와 관심의 불균형을 부각하는 패턴
examples: A quiet revolution is playing out in mental health care, a sector that serves millions yet receives a fraction of the national health budget.
A new trend is playing out in digital art, a sector that generates billions yet receives scant legal protection.

drillQuestions.question: A different kind of cultural shift is ______ out in the economics of intimate services.

sentences.notes: 동격어에 yet으로 역접을 넣어 경제적 규모와 학문적 무관심의 대비를 한 문장에 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A different kind of cultural shift is playing out
koreanAlignment.ko: 또 다른 종류의 문화적 변화가 전개되고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the economics of intimate services
generates an estimated one hundred billion dollars annually
receives remarkably little attention from researchers
koreanAlignment.ko: 연구자들로부터 놀라울 정도로 적은 관심을 받다

sentences.original: A single subscription platform hosting millions of adult content creators processes over seven billion dollars in annual spending, yet fewer than five percent of the academic literature on the broader industry over the past quarter century has adopted an economic lens.
sentences.translation: 수백만 명의 성인 콘텐츠 제작자를 호스팅하는 단일 구독 플랫폼이 연간 70억 달러 이상의 지출을 처리하고 있지만, 지난 사반세기 동안 이 광범위한 산업에 관한 학술 문헌 중 경제적 관점을 채택한 것은 5퍼센트도 되지 않는다.

grammarElements.text: A single subscription platform hosting millions of adult content creators
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 현재분사 수식구

grammarElements.text: over seven billion dollars in annual spending
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: yet fewer than five percent of the academic literature ... has adopted an economic lens
grammarElements.label: 역접절

vocabulary.example: More scholars need to adopt a historical lens when studying technology.
The country has undergone dramatic changes over the past quarter century.

expressions.usage: 극히 적은 비율을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 규모와 관심의 불균형을 yet으로 극적으로 대비하는 패턴
examples: The gaming industry generates more revenue than film and music combined, yet fewer than ten percent of universities offer dedicated game design programs.
The gig economy employs millions of workers, yet fewer than a quarter have access to health insurance.

drillQuestions.question: A single subscription platform processes over seven billion dollars, yet fewer than five percent of the academic literature has adopted an economic ______.

sentences.notes: 현재분사 'hosting millions'이 주어를 수식하며, yet 뒤에 학술적 무관심의 통계를 배치해 충격적 대비를 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: A single subscription platform hosting millions of adult content creators
koreanAlignment.ko: 수백만 명의 성인 콘텐츠 제작자를 호스팅하는 단일 구독 플랫폼

koreanAlignment.en: processes over seven billion dollars in annual spending
fewer than five percent of the academic literature
has adopted an economic lens

sentences.original: Digital platforms have begun to transform this market, lowering barriers to entry and making aspects of the trade more visible to study.
sentences.translation: 디지털 플랫폼이 이 시장을 변환하기 시작했으며, 진입 장벽을 낮추고 거래의 여러 측면을 연구하기에 더 가시적으로 만들고 있다.

grammarElements.text: lowering barriers to entry and making aspects of the trade more visible to study
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The high cost of equipment creates significant barriers to entry for new filmmakers.

expressions.usage: 숨겨졌던 현상이 학문적 조사가 가능해졌음을 나타냄

patterns.pattern: [Technology] have begun to transform [market], [lowering/reducing] [barriers] and [making/creating] [new possibilities]
patterns.explanation: 기술이 시장을 변화시키는 이중 효과를 분사구문으로 나열하는 패턴
examples: AI tools have begun to transform legal services, lowering costs for clients and making basic advice accessible to all.
Streaming platforms have begun to transform music distribution, lowering barriers for independent artists and making global audiences reachable.

drillQuestions.question: Digital platforms have begun to transform this market, lowering ______ to entry.

sentences.notes: 분사구문 lowering ... and making ...으로 디지털 전환의 이중 효과를 간결하게 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Digital platforms have begun to transform this market
koreanAlignment.ko: 디지털 플랫폼이 이 시장을 변환하기 시작했다

koreanAlignment.en: lowering barriers to entry
making aspects of the trade more visible to study
koreanAlignment.ko: 거래의 측면을 연구하기에 더 가시적으로 만들다

sentences.original: Researchers have borrowed creative methods from ecology, including tagging and recapturing participants at known locations, to estimate population sizes in the absence of reliable official data.
sentences.translation: 연구자들은 신뢰할 수 있는 공식 데이터가 없는 상황에서 인구 규모를 추정하기 위해, 알려진 장소에서 참여자를 표식하고 재포획하는 것을 포함한 생태학의 창의적 방법론을 차용했다.

grammarElements.text: including tagging and recapturing participants at known locations
grammarElements.label: 삽입 수식구

grammarElements.text: to estimate population sizes in the absence of reliable official data
grammarElements.label: 목적 to부정사구

vocabulary.example: Scientists recaptured the tagged birds to track their migration patterns.
In the absence of clear guidelines, managers relied on their own judgment.

expressions.usage: 학제 간 방법론 이전을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Researchers] have borrowed [methods] from [field], including [specific technique], to [research goal]
patterns.explanation: 다른 학문 분야의 방법론을 차용해 연구 목표를 달성하는 학제 간 접근법을 설명하는 패턴
examples: Economists have borrowed network analysis from sociology, including mapping relationship clusters, to understand financial contagion.
Urban planners have borrowed simulation models from epidemiology, including agent-based modeling, to predict traffic flows.

drillQuestions.question: Researchers have borrowed creative methods from ecology to estimate population sizes in the ______ of reliable official data.

sentences.notes: 생태학의 표식-재포획법(mark-recapture)을 사회과학에 적용한 창의적 학제 간 연구 사례이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Researchers have borrowed creative methods from ecology
tagging and recapturing participants at known locations
to estimate population sizes
in the absence of reliable official data
koreanAlignment.ko: 신뢰할 수 있는 공식 데이터가 없는 상황에서

sentences.original: Natural experiments created by changing regulations have also yielded insights, with studies finding that decriminalization tends to reduce both violent crime and disease transmission.
sentences.translation: 규제 변화에 의해 만들어진 자연 실험도 통찰을 제공했는데, 연구들은 비범죄화가 폭력 범죄와 질병 전파를 모두 줄이는 경향이 있다는 것을 발견했다.

grammarElements.text: Natural experiments created by changing regulations
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 과거분사 수식구

grammarElements.text: with studies finding that decriminalization tends to reduce both violent crime and disease transmission
grammarElements.label: 부대상황 전치사구

vocabulary.example: The experiment yielded unexpected but valuable results.
The decriminalization of cannabis has been debated in many countries.

expressions.usage: 실험실이 아닌 현실의 정책 변화 등으로 인한 자연발생적 실험 상황을 가리킴

patterns.pattern: Natural experiments created by [cause] have yielded insights, with studies finding that [conclusion]
patterns.explanation: 자연 실험의 결과를 보고하면서 구체적 발견을 부대상황 구문으로 제시하는 패턴
examples: Natural experiments created by sudden lockdowns have yielded insights, with studies finding that air quality improved dramatically within weeks.
Natural experiments created by border closures have yielded insights, with studies finding that local supply chains proved more resilient than expected.

drillQuestions.question: Natural experiments created by changing regulations have also ______ insights.
Studies finding that decriminalization tends to reduce both violent crime and disease ______.

sentences.notes: with studies finding that ...은 독립분사구문으로, 학술 글쓰기에서 부가적 증거를 제시할 때 즐겨 쓰인다.

koreanAlignment.en: Natural experiments created by changing regulations
koreanAlignment.ko: 규제 변화에 의해 만들어진 자연 실험

koreanAlignment.en: have also yielded insights
decriminalization tends to reduce
both violent crime and disease transmission

sentences.original: These three developments reflect a broader pattern in which social norms, emotional wellbeing, and economic activity are evolving faster than the institutions designed to measure and govern them.
sentences.translation: 이 세 가지 발전은 사회적 규범, 정서적 안녕, 그리고 경제 활동이 이를 측정하고 통치하도록 설계된 제도보다 더 빠르게 진화하고 있는 보다 광범위한 패턴을 반영한다.

grammarElements.text: in which social norms, emotional wellbeing, and economic activity are evolving faster than the institutions designed to measure and govern them
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: Consumer preferences are evolving faster than companies can adapt.
The laws that govern digital privacy are outdated.

expressions.phrase: evolving faster than the institutions designed to measure and govern them
expressions.meaning: 이를 측정하고 통치하도록 설계된 제도보다 더 빠르게 진화하다
expressions.usage: 변화 속도가 제도적 대응 능력을 초과함을 표현

patterns.pattern: These [number] developments reflect a broader pattern in which [elements] are [verbing] faster than [institutional response]
patterns.explanation: 여러 현상을 하나의 거시적 패턴으로 묶어 결론짓는 마무리 패턴
examples: These four crises reflect a broader pattern in which technology is advancing faster than the regulations designed to contain it.
These two trends reflect a broader pattern in which demographic change is outpacing the welfare systems built for a different era.

drillQuestions.question: These three developments reflect a broader ______ in which social norms are evolving faster than institutions.

sentences.notes: 세 주제를 하나의 거시적 통찰로 종합하는 에세이 결론부의 전형적 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: These three developments reflect a broader pattern
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 세 가지 발전은 보다 광범위한 패턴을 반영한다

koreanAlignment.en: social norms, emotional wellbeing, and economic activity
koreanAlignment.ko: 사회적 규범, 정서적 안녕, 그리고 경제 활동

koreanAlignment.en: are evolving faster than
the institutions designed to measure and govern them
